I waited a full year before writing this review, hoping time would soften my frustration or offer clarity. It hasn’t. Facts are facts, and my experience with Haugen Law Firm remains deeply disappointing.

I was referred to this firm by their own family, which made the experience even more disheartening. I came to Haugen Law for a straightforward prenuptial agreement—organized, prepared, and with clear expectations. Yet, I was charged over $4,200 for what should have been a routine legal matter. The billing was bloated, opaque, and riddled with questionable charges. Administrative tasks like file setup and maintenance—standard overhead in any professional office—were billed separately. Internal strategy meetings and supervision among attorneys were passed on to me as if I should foot the bill for their inefficiencies and training.

Despite multiple inquiries, I received vague justifications and no accountability. The firm’s response was a masterclass in deflection. I was told that delegating work to junior staff was meant to save me money—yet I was charged for nearly every internal conversation, instruction, and file update. If a less experienced attorney needed constant supervision, that’s a cost the firm should absorb, not the client.

Even after expressing my concerns and agreeing to a specific date and time to discuss them, Conner Haugen failed to call. I waited, and he simply didn’t show. That level of disregard speaks volumes. His later excuse—a client emergency—didn’t change the fact that I was left waiting without notice or follow-up until I reached out.

We had planned to use Haugen Law for post-modification representation and estate planning. However, due to my experience, we sought out another firm—one that is transparent, respectful, and financially responsible.

If you’re considering Haugen Law Firm, I strongly urge you to look elsewhere. Professionalism and integrity should be non-negotiable. Unfortunately, my experience proved they are optional here.

I spent a significant amount of time researching attorneys with military experience to handle my divorce, as I knew it was important to have someone who truly understood the unique challenges that come with military life. Choosing to work with Mr. Haugen turned out to be the best decision I could have made. From start to finish, I genuinely felt that he had my best interests at heart.

Mr. Haugen provided thoughtful and strategic advice every step of the way, always clear and informed, yet never pushy. His calm demeanor helped ease my stress during an otherwise difficult time, and he was consistently responsive and incredibly flexible in accommodating my needs.

His entire team reflects the same professionalism and compassion, including his paralegal, Mrs. Delaney, and Mr. Connor. I couldn’t have asked for a better legal team to guide me through this chapter. Thanks!!

I used John Haugen and his son Connor for an enforcement action in family court. Before I hired the Haugen firm, I was convinced my problem was too big for anyone to handle. It’s really difficult to go to family court for any reason. John Haugen evaluated my case and gave me an idea of what to expect and whether it was worth pursuing.

Historically, the other party had refused to cooperate and failed to transfer significant assets that I was  previously awarded. It was an extremely frustrating situation. I’d like to say that with the Haugen firm everything was magically fixed overnight but the wheels of justices do take awhile especially with a stubborn respondent and Covid delaying the courts. That’s a result of Covid and not something Haugen’s firm had control of. Some of the hearings were live in court and some where via Zoom.

Haugen’s team kept me informed about the progress of my case or rescheduling of the court. That happened a lot! Covid took everyone by surprise. However at the end of the enforcement, I prevailed with the transfer of the assets in question and my attorney fees, paid by the other party. My enforcement got to such a ridiculous extent that the next step for the judge may have been incarceration. I’m told that is a very rare occurrence to even get to that point.

I really liked that John originally sat down with the other party to explain the situation and what he needed first. I wanted amicable compliance if possible. However, when the other party was uncooperative time after time, I saw that John and his son are a formidable force. They were professional to all they dealt with and got the result I wanted.

I decided to go into a bit of detail with my review for a reason. I wanted to give others an idea of what a somewhat messy family case is like. I also wanted to highlight the Haugen’s professionalism and results. You may be scared, you might have convinced yourself you’ll fail before you get started. It’s worth your time to get a competent evaluation of your case from an experienced attorney.

reviews.text: Being someone who had never needed to consider filing bankruptcy I found Shuster Law here on Google. I cant thank David and his team enough and if I ever find myself in a situation that requires legal representation within their firm's areas of expertise they will be the first call I make.

Great for the initial bankruptcy, not great when a creditor started reporting on my credit again.

Everything with the bankruptcy went fine. It was filed in July and then done in October.  I was assured that this was the only way to not be liable for my ex-husbands mortgage, which was the only reason I did a bankruptcy.

2 years later the mortgage company reports the mortgage back on my credit. They had started foreclosure proceedings, and my name was on the lawsuit.

I called the office and first I was referred by the gal who answered the phone to a credit repair office. I called again because they had told me when I filed that if any of the creditors began reporting those accounts again, it would be breaking the law and to contact them.

I spoke to a paralegal who listened to what was going on, and he said it definitely was against the law. He said he would have a retainer sent over so they could start the process.  I never received the retainer. I reached out twice after that and they said they'd send it over. Finally after 2 weeks, I found a different attorney to help me and get the whole situation resolved.

Two things should have happened.  They should have told me that the bankruptcy wouldn't completely remove me from anything to do with that mortgage.  And they should tell people up front when they are too busy instead of wasting their time.
reviews.ownerResponse: I thought I already responded to this.  A bankruptcy discharge will extinguish your debt. If a creditor is not compliant, then we would have instructed you what to do. Ultimately, we referred these cases to someone who specifically focuses on, not necessarily credit repair but suing creditors who break the law by reporting on your credit report after a bankruptcy.

Terrible Experience – Only Care About Billing, Not Clients!

Hiring North Texas Attorney was one of the worst decisions I’ve ever made. From day one, it became clear that all they cared about was billing every two weeks, not helping me or adding any real value to my case. Despite paying regularly, there was little to no progress. They constantly came up with excuses or unnecessary “tasks” just to justify another invoice.

My assigned attorney, James Giries, even missed a critical hearing that I hired them specifically to handle because he went on vacation, and no one from the firm took responsibility. I made every effort to ensure that hearing occurred, but they completely failed to show up. Afterward, the firm kept sending bills through Priscila Maldonado, who always made sure new invoices came in, even when nothing meaningful was done.

Because of their negligence, the court eventually dismissed the suit I filed and moved straight to final trial. That’s when I realized I had to take control of my case. I immediately fired them and decided to represent myself as a pro se, and I actually achieved better results on my own than they ever did.

Looking back, I regret ignoring the negative reviews before hiring them. I thought maybe those reviews were exaggerated until I experienced the exact same thing myself. They prolong your case just to keep billing you and drain your finances. When you can’t pay anymore, they drop your case.

If you want to waste money, time, and energy while your case goes nowhere, hire them. But if you truly care about your case and your peace of mind, run from this firm. I have documentation and proof of everything I’ve stated here. This experience taught me a major lesson, never again.

reviews.text:
Consultation with Chad went great and I thought he was going to have my back. Hired, then had one five minute conversation before he filed an answer that was lacking in the things we discussed at the initial consult as well as asking for things (like a jury trial) that was never discussed or approved. He filed my answer without conferring with me or getting final approval. In fact it was not even uploaded to me until about a week later, despite multiple attempts at contact.

Genuinely just didn’t seem to care. After a week of trying to get in contact multiple different ways, he canceled my hearing prep meeting for a TRO that was happening the next day. This man wanted me to show up at a TRO Hearing with only one five minute conversation and 1 consultation under our belts. Absolutely not. I was forced to get a continuance and go three extra months with no resolution to the frivolous suit filed against me.

Ate up my entire retainer after filing less than 1 page of an answer before we even got to the hearing I hired him for. He withdrew from my case after I got frustrated with his paralegal who told me multiple times she would call me back and never did. She also gave me incorrect hearing dates. She also did not seem to care.

Furthermore, BEFORE the judge signed off on his withdrawal the firm failed to send over the opposing party’s discovery when received or let me know when mine was due, nor did he forward me the Notice of Hearing that was filed and served to his office exclusively. Horrible, borderline unethical practice.

+1 star for filing a semi coherent answer and for showing me what a good practice SHOULDN'T do. Shame on me for not seeing through the facade.

Luckily I found someone else who showed me how a good firm operates and got me exactly the outcome I wanted.

I'm sort of confused on how to interpret this review. I was highly pleased with the initial meeting I had and the passion/expertise regarding my case. However, I'm conflicted and feel scammed because I have made several phone calls and messages regarding my inability to view the documents and invoices that are uploaded to my case. However, I had no problem with someone contacting me initially to make sure I can sign the docs and submit my payment online, but after the payment was received no communication efforts were made regarding the messages and calls I made regarding my inability to access mycase (the online portal to view docs and invoices). It's been several weeks, keep in mind this started in December so I am considerate of the holiday season causing some delays. But there is no excuse for it to be January 10th and I have yet to have any efforts made to send me my docs or assist me with setting up a new account with a different email in order to access the online portal to see what's going on.
One time I was able to speak with Casey I believe and she claimed she would send me the documents that I have via email since I couldn't use the online portal. However it never happened and I am still trying to see how was my case handled, what are the charges that are/may be deducted from my deposit. I shouldn't be left in the dark for so long.
I'm truly disappointed and feel concerned about what's happening behind the scenes and what is/was the intent.
I find it quite suspicious that someone initialized communication to make sure I can sign and submit payment but once the deposit was received; I have no response or call backs with my concerns about not being able to access my docs and invoices via the portal. I hope this is only an issue for me and no one else. I really thought I found a law office that I could trust and work with in the future. I don't know what to think at this point.

reviews.text: Hire Ryan Thornhill! You’re reading lawyer reviews because you’re looking for the right lawyer, but you really need to look no further than Ryan. The truth is, lawyers are a dime a dozen. I know because I’m a lawyer. But, I don’t practice family law. So, I know next to nothing about family law, but Ryan does! He and his paralegal, Kandis, are a formidable team! They each possess something that is very rare, especially among lawyers, and that is this: knowledge of the law and compassion for their clients. I know I was not their only client, but they always made me feel like I was. Going through a divorce was the worst experience of my life: full of fear and pain and uncertainty…. But Ryan and Kandis got me through it all. I just can’t say enough good things about them. So instead, I will just say this, HIRE RYAN!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your thoughtful and glowing review! It truly means a lot, especially coming from someone who understands the legal profession so well. Ryan Thornhill and our team are deeply committed to providing exceptional service, and your words affirm that dedication. We are thrilled to have earned your trust and confidence. If you ever need assistance again, please do not hesitate to reach out—we are always here to help!

From my experience, divorce proceedings, even without having to go to court, seemed like a small war, with unavoidable emotional, collateral damage, especially with kids involved.
But, with the help of the Nelson Law Group’s entire team, in particular attorneys Brett Nelson and Nancy Bouajram, I came out the other side with a minimum of scathing. (I had used another firm first, and wish I’d started and finished with Brett and Nancy).
Not only did they provide legal help, but advice from a quarter century of practice, and empathized with my particular family needs, and financial situation. While it did cost some money, at the end, I came out with a much better settlement than I’d hoped for. I never felt that I was being “gouged,” as I had with a previous firm. I wish I’d come here first.
While there was bitterness on my part about getting divorced, Brett counseled me at our first meeting to take the high road when speaking with my kids. There have been times when his saying that has kept me on that path. When I was really down, Nancy would say, “There IS life after divorce.” Perhaps as a result of their advice, while my ex and I are certainly not buddybuddy, we can get along, especially where the kids are concerned, and have even traveled together twice with the kids.
As you won’t be their only client, I learned to write bullet point type emails highlighting questions, concerns, and key needs. Brett and Nancy were excellent in addressing such emails, point for point, so that we were on the same page. It’s also the most cost efficient way to go.
In finale, on the date of the divorce decree, Brett protected me for myself – by stopping me from making an unnecessary financial concession.
Despite going through the hell of a divorce, after being led through the dark by Brett and Nancy, I would absolutely STILL get married again were the right situation to arise.
John LaVine
